title: Oldenlandia tenella
taxonomy: Oldenlandia tenella belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a part of the genus Oldenlandia, which comprises various species of herbs and shrubs. Oldenlandia tenella is native to Asia and is commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is classified as a herbaceous species, characterized by its delicate stems and leaves. Within the Rubiaceae family, Oldenlandia tenella is classified as a member of the subfamily Rubioideae.
uses: Oldenlandia tenella is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating infections and wounds. Additionally, Oldenlandia tenella is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, its effectiveness and safety for human consumption should be further studied and confirmed by scientific research.

intro: Oldenlandia tenella, commonly known as small broom bush or tenella, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia tenella include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Oldenlandia tenella is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of various herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
